Treatment Class.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) combined with an androgen axis inhibitor (orteronel) or bicalutamide
Treatment Class.reasoning: The PURPOSE section states that orteronel is a 'nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis' and was evaluated 'when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. The METHODS section specifies that patients were assigned to 'ADT with orteronel' or 'ADT with bicalutamide'. The CONTEXT section refers to orteronel as a 'novel androgen axis inhibitor' added to ADT.

Add-on Treatment.reasoning: The abstract (f3439a17-e5bd-46ac-b6c7-dad1a6f46b40) states, "We evaluated the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)". The methods section (444aeda4-f92f-4800-a667-1b82802320e) further specifies that patients were randomly assigned to "ADT with orteronel (experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (control arm)". Orteronel is the primary add-on treatment being investigated in this trial.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.reasoning: The 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4 states: "The PSA response rates at month 7 were significantly improved with orteronel (CR 58%; PR: 22%, NR: 19%)". Overall Response Rate (ORR) is the sum of Complete Response (CR) and Partial Response (PR). For the orteronel arm, CR is 58% and PR is 22%, so ORR = 58% + 22% = 80%.
